hard.next_step: Confidence 25% 2nd Jul- RFP finally arrived- under review now- due 24th July. EB has a strong historic feeling against Zycus. 4th Oct face-to-face demo dominated by hostile VP; functional gaps flagged.

north_star_verdict.headline: The long-awaited RFP finally arrived 2 Jul (due 24 Jul), keeping Fly Dubai alive, but a senior procurement VP who deployed rival tools at Emirates and Jumeirah is convinced Zycus failed in the region, we hold no champion, and four functional gaps sit open against JAGGAER.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 3 Nov 2025 (245 days ago). Advanced Initial Interest to Qualified on 2 Jul 2026, so only 4 days in Qualified. 177 days to the 30 Dec 2026 close (pushed twice this year: 30 Sep to 30 Dec). RFP arrived 2 Jul, response due 24 Jul (18 days out). A nine-month engagement gap separates the 4 Oct 2025 demo from the 2 Jul RFP. To hit the close the deal must clear RFP, shortlist, vendor selection, negotiation and contracting in 177 days against a hostile economic buyer with no champion; pace is aggressive, but the formal RFP invitation keeps the deal live rather than cold.
evidence: RFP arrived 2 Jul 2026, due 24 Jul, now under review (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026)
'the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason' (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026)

4 Oct 2025 demo 'dominated by the VP (very experienced ex-Emirates/Jumeriah Group senior decision maker, who has deployed most other competitors)...strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region and was removed from both organisations' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025)
Functional gaps: supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, recommending external vendors/pricing models, 'they can get from the competition' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025)
'should seriously consider qualifying out if we are invited' (Next Step, 12 Jan 2026)
No Contact Role flagged as Champion; controlling VP not named in Salesforce
Amount raised 165k to 265k and close pushed 30 Sep to 30 Dec, both on 2 Jul 2026 by Carl Kimball (field history)
Owner changed Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (Regional VP) on 1 Jul 2026 (field history)
Competitor JAGGAER named in Salesforce; buyer 'held sessions with all the competition beforehand' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025)
Zero Avoma calls found for this opportunity; evidence is Salesforce Next Step, field history and email activity only

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. The buyer framed the engagement as 'initial market research' and a 'project likely in 2026' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025), and named operational gaps (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, external vendor recommendation) but never attached a cost, cycle-time, headcount or savings figure to them. There is no AIS score and no compelling event on record. Without a dollar or efficiency baseline Zycus cannot anchor ROI against JAGGAER or counter the VP's belief that Zycus delivers less value, so quantifying the pain with the procurement excellence team before the 24 Jul RFP response is the ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is a senior procurement VP, described as 'very experienced ex-Emirates/Jumeriah Group senior decision maker, who has deployed most other competitors', who dominated the 4 Oct 2025 demo (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025). He is identified by role and behaviour but is NOT named in any Salesforce contact role, so he cannot be verified by name here. He is actively hostile: 'strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region and was removed from both organisations' (4 Oct 2025), and 'the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason' (2 Jul 2026). The named Salesforce Decision Maker, Nawaf Al Awadhi (Director of Commercial Sourcing), has no recorded engagement. The move is to get this VP named and, given the historic grievance, front a regional reference and a fresh proof point rather than another feature demo.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Nawaf Al Awadhi, Decision Maker

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are visible from the 4 Oct 2025 demo feedback: the buyer weighs supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission and the ability to recommend external vendors and pricing models, and explicitly noted 'they can get [these] from the competition' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025). Regional track record is a hard criterion in the VP's mind given his conviction that Zycus 'failed in the region'. The formal criteria will be codified in the RFP that arrived 2 Jul (due 24 Jul), which is under review. The move is to map the RFP requirements line-by-line to Merlin-driven automation on the four gap areas and to a Middle-East reference before submission.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, multi-vendor procurement: they 'held sessions with all the competition beforehand' and used the 4 Oct 2025 face-to-face demo as market research, then floated an RFP that arrived 2 Jul 2026 with a 24 Jul response deadline (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025 and 2 Jul 2026). The process is procurement-led and gated by the RFP round leading into shortlisting. The controlling VP dominates the room; Wasim Almasri (Category Manager IT and Digital Procurement) is the current RFP contact per the 2 Jul acknowledgment email, and Elvis Dsouza was the early point of contact. Approval chain beyond the VP and Nawaf Al Awadhi (Director of Commercial Sourcing) is not yet documented; clarifying the evaluation stages and shortlist timeline is the move.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are on record: no MSA, SOW, InfoSec, procurement-channel or contract-expiration detail has been captured, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. The RFP (arrived 2 Jul, due 24 Jul) is the first formal procurement artifact and will likely specify commercial terms and evaluation gates. The full-suite scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk) will eventually require implementation scoping and ERP integration, but at Qualified stage this is early. The move is to extract the RFP's commercial and timeline requirements to build backward-looking visibility into the paper process.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer articulated concrete operational pain at the 4 Oct 2025 demo: gaps in supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, and recommending external vendors and pricing models (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025). These are real workflow inefficiencies, but they are framed as capabilities the buyer wants, not as quantified cost or cycle-time pain, and the eight-month gap to the 2 Jul RFP signals the pain is not urgent. The pain owner appears to sit with the procurement excellence and IT procurement contacts (Elvis Dsouza, Wasim Almasri), while the VP owns the strategic view. No compelling event has been named. The move is to convert these qualitative gaps into a quantified, RFP-anchored value case.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Elvis Dsouza, Wasim Almasri

champion.narrative: No champion exists. No Contact Role is flagged as Champion, and no contact has shown advocacy. Elvis Dsouza (Procurement Specialist - Business Intelligence, Evaluator) initiated contact after the Dubai World Tour event (Next Step, 24 Sep 2025) and was the early email point of contact (Jan 2026 RFP chase emails), but acts as a coordinator, not an advocate. Wasim Almasri (Category Manager IT and Digital Procurement) is the current RFP contact per the 2 Jul acknowledgment email but has no attributed advocacy. The controlling VP is hostile and Nawaf Al Awadhi (Decision Maker) is silent, so there is no one navigating the VP's objection internally. The ranked move is to earn Wasim or Elvis as a coach on how to address the VP's historic grievance before the RFP response goes in.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Elvis Dsouza; Wasim Almasri
Task 2 Jul 2026 RFP email to Wasim

competition.narrative: JAGGAER is the named competitor (SF Competitors__c) and the incumbent frame of reference for the economic buyer, who 'has deployed most other competitors' at Emirates and Jumeirah Group and is 'strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025). The buyer 'held sessions with all the competition beforehand', so this is a full multi-vendor evaluation now formalised through the RFP. The four functional gaps Zycus showed at the demo are capabilities the buyer says 'they can get from the competition', which hands JAGGAER a feature-parity and trust advantage. The wedge is Merlin: position AI-driven supplier discovery, automated onboarding and intelligent vendor recommendation to leapfrog JAGGAER's manual workflows, paired with a Middle-East reference that directly rebuts the VP's 'failed in the region' conviction.

deal_movement.summary: After a nine-month quiet stretch, the deal reactivated in early July 2026: the long-awaited RFP arrived 2 Jul (due 24 Jul), the record advanced Initial Interest to Qualified, ownership moved to Regional VP Mohamad Alhakim, amount was raised to 265k, and the close was pushed to 30 Dec 2026.

items.change: RFP arrived and is under review, response due 24 Jul
Zycus emailed Wasim Almasri acknowledging receipt of the RFP, naming Mohamad Alhakim and Tanya Rajesh as forward contacts
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified (by Carl Kimball)
Close date pushed 30 Sep to 30 Dec 2026 (by Carl Kimball)
Owner reassigned Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim, Regional VP (by Ishwarya Anbarasan)
RFP-status chase email to Elvis Dsouza asking if delayed or not invited
Face-to-face demo at Fly Dubai HQ dominated by hostile VP; functional gaps flagged

competitive_position.summary: JAGGAER is the single strongest current threat: it is the named competitor and the buying VP's trusted reference point, having deployed 'most other competitors' at Emirates and Jumeirah, and it covers the four functional gaps Zycus showed at the 4 Oct 2025 demo. The evaluation is a formal multi-vendor RFP (buyer 'held sessions with all the competition'), and the VP is actively leaning away from Zycus on both feature parity and a historic regional grievance. Zycus is behind and must lead with Merlin automation plus a Middle-East reference to reset the field.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fly Dubai's stated expectations centre on supplier-side capabilities and regional credibility, both areas where Zycus is currently exposed against JAGGAER and the buyer's prior experience.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a Qualified, competitor-led RFP with a hostile economic buyer and no champion; winning hinges on neutralising the VP's grievance, earning an internal coach, and differentiating on Merlin before the 24 Jul submission.
flags: Directly counter the VP's 'Zycus failed in the region' conviction with a live Middle-East aviation or enterprise reference in the RFP response, not another feature demo.
Earn a coach: enlist Wasim Almasri or Elvis Dsouza to reveal the VP's real objections and shape how the response addresses the four functional gaps.
Differentiate on Merlin AI (supplier discovery, automated onboarding, vendor recommendation) to leapfrog JAGGAER's manual workflows rather than competing on feature parity you will lose.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Elvis Dsouza and Wasim Almasri are process contacts, not advocates, and the controlling VP is hostile while the named Decision Maker Nawaf Al Awadhi is silent. Without an internal coach to navigate the VP's grievance and shape the RFP response, the deal cannot progress.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; SF Next_Step__c 4 Oct 2025, 2 Jul 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in the product scope and is the logical wedge to leapfrog JAGGAER's manual supplier discovery, onboarding and bid workflows, but it has not been positioned to the buyer and no AI narrative has been put in front of them; Zycus is under-positioned on its strongest differentiator.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score is captured and the buyer has expressed no explicit AI appetite; Merlin sits in the product scope but the four gaps they raised are automation-adjacent, so an AI-led automation story is a credible, untested angle rather than a confirmed pull.

items.detail: The economic-buyer VP holds a historic grievance and believes Zycus failed in the region and was removed from Emirates and Jumeirah; he dominated the demo and is hostile.
Four functional gaps (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, external-vendor/pricing recommendation) the competition already covers.
No champion and a silent Decision Maker leave Zycus with no one to counter the VP internally; single-thread into process contacts only.
No quantified value case or compelling event; buyer framed the effort as market research, so budget urgency is unproven despite a 265k figure.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement reactivated with the 2 Jul RFP after a nine-month gap, but the rep's own note advises considering qualifying out; signal quality is thin (no calls, single-thread) and dependent on the RFP outcome.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Fly Dubai's RFP finally landed 2 Jul (due 24 Jul) after a nine-month silence, but a hostile senior procurement VP who deployed rival tools at Emirates and Jumeirah rates Zycus below JAGGAER, we hold no champion, and four functional gaps remain open, so we are in the RFP but well behind.

win_position.text: This deal sits low: a Qualified opportunity where the senior procurement VP who dominated the 4 Oct 2025 demo is convinced Zycus 'failed in the region and was removed' from Emirates and Jumeirah, and no counter-champion exists (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).
JAGGAER is the buyer's trusted reference point: the VP has deployed 'most other competitors' and can source the four capabilities Zycus lacks from them (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).
The positive: Zycus was formally invited to the RFP that arrived 2 Jul 2026 (due 24 Jul), so we remain in the evaluation despite the headwinds (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The RFP arrived 2 Jul 2026 with a firm 24 Jul submission deadline, the first concrete buyer-driven milestone since the 4 Oct 2025 demo (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026).
Momentum was near-zero for nine months: no buyer engagement between the 4 Oct 2025 demo and the 2 Jul RFP, and the deal only advanced to Qualified on 2 Jul (field history).
The rep's own note advises 'should seriously consider qualifying out if we are invited', signalling fragile momentum (Next Step, 12 Jan 2026).

customer_commitment.text: No champion and no economic-buyer sponsorship: the controlling VP is actively hostile and the named Decision Maker Nawaf Al Awadhi (Director of Commercial Sourcing) has never engaged (contact roles; Next Step).
Commitment is procurement-driven market research, not a funded mandate: the buyer 'held sessions with all the competition' and called it 'initial market research' (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).
Budget was signalled with 'budget and rough timelines into 2026' and the amount was raised to 265k on 2 Jul (Next Step 4 Oct 2025; field history).

deal_risk.text: Economic-buyer hostility rooted in a historic grievance: 'the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason' (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026).
Four functional gaps (supplier discovery, supplier onboarding/login, email bid submission, external-vendor recommendation) the competition already covers (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).
du.ae-domain contacts (Nawaf Al Awadhi, Haissam Ali Hassan) alongside flydubai.com contacts leave the buying entity and decision unit unclear (contact roles).

champion_strength.evidence: No Contact Role is a champion; process contacts only and a hostile VP (SF OpportunityContactRole; Next Step 4 Oct 2025).

executive_alignment.evidence: Economic-buyer VP hostile on a historic grievance (Next Step 2 Jul 2026).

competitor_position.evidence: JAGGAER favoured by the VP and covers the four gaps Zycus lacks (Next Step 4 Oct 2025).

scope_change.detail: Amount raised 165k to 265k on 2 Jul 2026 by Carl Kimball alongside the RFP arrival; full six-module suite (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk) remains in scope (field history).

customer_preference.evidence: The economic-buyer VP is 'strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region', and prefers competitors he has deployed (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).

business_case.evidence: Only qualitative capability gaps named; no cost, cycle-time, savings or ROI quantified, and no compelling event (Next Step, 4 Oct 2025).

critical_signals.text: JAGGAER is favoured because the controlling procurement VP deployed it and rival tools at Emirates and Jumeirah and rates Zycus weaker, and it covers the four capabilities Zycus lacks.
The long-awaited RFP finally arrived on 2 Jul with a 24 Jul response deadline, the first real re-engagement since the 4 Oct demo nine months earlier.
The buyer needs supplier discovery, supplier onboarding/login, email bid submission and external-vendor recommendation, all flagged as gaps they can source from the competition.
The deal was resized to 265k for a full six-module suite, but there is no quantified ROI and the economic buyer doubts Zycus delivers value, so the price is not yet justified.
